steps.title: Identify complementary angles and apply identities
steps.content:
Notice that $24° + 66° = 90°$, making these angles complementary. For complementary angles, we use the identities:
$$\cos66° = \sin24°$$
$$\sin66° = \cos24°$$

steps.title: Substitute the identities into the expression
steps.content:
Replace $\cos66°$ with $\sin24°$ and $\sin66°$ with $\cos24°$ in the original expression:
$$(\sin24°)(\cos66°) + (\cos24°)(\sin66°) = (\sin24°)(\sin24°) + (\cos24°)(\cos24°)$$
Simplifying:
$$(\sin24°)^2 + (\cos24°)^2$$

steps.title: Rewrite in terms of the choices
steps.content:
Since $\sin24° = \cos66°$, we can rewrite our expression as:
$$(\cos66°)^2 + (\cos24°)^2$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
